author: William of Melitona/Middleton
title: Opusculum super missam
editions: Opusculum super Missam, ed. W. Lampen, Ephemerides Liturgicae 43 (1929), 329–345, 392–409; Opusculum super Missam, ed. W. Lampen (Ad Claras Aquas, Florence, 1931 (second edition)); Opusculum super Missam, ed. A. van Dijk, Ephemerides Liturgicae 53 (1939), 291–349 & 54 (1940), 3–11 (an edition based on different manuscripts).
remarks: [see] See Roest, 362ff. "Whereas [Alexander]... dealt with the Mass in an intellectual manner and was aimed at a well-educated public, as did many Franciscan Sentences commentaries and academic quaestiones, William’s Opusculum super Missam tried to provide information to simple priests (simplicibus), and this not solely for their instruction, but also to instill further piety."
